field_decisions.reason: The surviving regulatory and substitute evidence is safely framed at EU level.

field_decisions.value: Article 4 applies to providers and deployers of AI systems, requires context-sensitive AI literacy measures, does not mandate a one-size-fits-all training format, certificate, sector template, or governance structure, and already applies from 2 February 2025 with enforcement beginning in August 2026.
field_decisions.reason: This is the narrowest safe synthesis of the Commission guidance.
The rerun directly verified both a paid French alternative and a free multilingual EU-wide alternative.

value: Legacy all-companies scope copy is wrong; Article 4 is not a blanket requirement for every EU company
The English-only market-gap claim is not established after finding French and multilingual substitutes
EU-wide remote delivery as a complete solution is unproven without country- and context-specific evidence
The FR/DE/EN positioning is not yet a durable moat because Elements of AI already covers those languages
Any product-specific certificate, documentation, or governance-artifact value remains unverified beyond the Commission's baseline
field_decisions.reason: These are the concrete fail-closed reasons the product stays in review.

field_decisions.value: EU providers and deployers of AI systems, especially HR L&D, compliance, and operating teams that need a practical Article 4 literacy layer.
field_decisions.omission_reason: This is a useful synthesis from the verified regulatory context, but it is still a positioning inference rather than a default dashboard fact.

field_decisions.value: The official guidance points toward context-sensitive implementation, so country and use-case adaptation may matter more than the product's current EU-wide generic framing implies.
field_decisions.omission_reason: This is an analyst interpretation of the surviving evidence, not a direct dashboard-safe fact.
The Commission source contradicts any blanket all-companies scope statement.
No reviewed source proves that a single remote EU-wide format fully solves buyer needs across contexts.
The rerun found both French and multilingual substitutes, so the language-based moat is not yet proven.
The reviewed guidance does not prove that any B3-issued certificate, documentation pack, or governance artifact carries independent compliance significance.
